desc: Generating a new dataset does not invalidate resultCache, so the UI can display analysis results belonging to the previous dataset.
analyzeData never clears resultCache, causing obsolete entries such as "error" or metrics from prior analyses to remain and be displayed alongside current results.
The Analyze button always calls updateStatus and reports analysis completion even when no data exists and analyzeData records an error.
Core data, cached results, and UI widgets are stored in mutable module globals, tightly coupling callbacks and making state consistency, testing, and reuse difficult.
Category counts repeatedly scan the entire category list once per distinct value via cats.count, yielding avoidable O(n*k) work instead of a single-pass count.
The mean and median are each computed twice for the same input, adding unnecessary work and duplicating logic that can drift during maintenance.
The Analyze callback uses a lambda that constructs a throwaway list solely to sequence side effects, obscuring control flow and error handling.
